title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what attitudes towards fashion and shopping they suggest and how consumerism affects people's lives.

images.label: A
images.description: A woman surrounded by dozens of shopping bags in a brightly lit mall, looking thrilled as she examines her purchases

images.label: B
images.description: A queue of people outside a flagship fashion store early in the morning, waiting for a limited edition collection to go on sale

images.label: C
images.description: A man carefully browsing rails of second-hand clothing in a charity shop, holding up a vintage jacket to check the fit

followUp.question: Do you think people place too much importance on fashion and appearance in today's society? Why or why not?

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what the challenges and benefits of adopting sustainable fashion practices might be.

images.label: D
images.description: A fashion designer in a small studio working with organic cotton fabrics and recycled materials, surrounded by mood boards featuring earthy tones

images.label: E
images.description: A market stall selling upcycled and repaired clothing, with a handwritten sign promoting 'slow fashion', busy with interested customers

images.label: F
images.description: Workers in a poorly lit factory producing cheap garments in large quantities, conveyor belts moving rapidly in the background

followUp.question: Do you think individual consumers or large fashion companies bear more responsibility for making the fashion industry sustainable?
